relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 77-year-old man with a history of coronary artery disease (including bypass surgery in 2009), chronic obstructive pulmonary disease on inhaled steroids, dyslipidaemia, and hypertension presented with persistent left-sided chest pain on exertion for one week and mild dyspnoea.
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Intramyocardial hematoma is a rare mechanical complication that can occur after PCI or MI, similar to the patient's extensive dissection. Matches key finding: intramural hematoma.

relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 49-year-old woman presented with worsening anginal chest pain over several weeks. Her blood pressure was 202/100 mm Hg and heart rate 102 beats/min; the rest of the physical examination was unrema
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Coronary dissection is a direct match to the patient's extensive dissection during PCI. Though spontaneous in this case, the mechanism involves arterial wall compromise similar to iatrogenic dissection.

relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 73-year-old man with stable angina pectoris was admitted for percutaneous coronary intervention of the proximal left anterior descending (LAD) artery. He had hypertension, was an ex-smoker, and had under
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Direct match: post-PCI complication with hemorrhagic shock during coronary intervention. This case demonstrates hemodynamic collapse during PCI, similar to the patient's presentation with refractory hypotension and hemoglobin drop.
